table_description: Insured policyholders — individuals or companies. Use index_name for the readable name.

table_columns.name: index_name
table_columns.description: Human-readable name, e.g. 'Patrick Myers', 'Summit Shield Risk Solutions'.

sample_queries.question: How many policies does Patrick Myers have?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(p.system_id) FROM customers c JOIN policies p ON c.system_id = p.customer_ref WHERE c.index_name LIKE '%Patrick Myers%'

sample_queries.question: Find all customers who are companies
sample_queries.sql: SELECT index_name, email FROM customers WHERE entity_type = 'Company'

rules: Customers are Insured persons/businesses. Not agencies.
